title: quassia bidwillii
intro: Quassia bidwillii, also known as the Australian Quassia or Bidwill's Quassia, is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the family Rutaceae. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bitter compounds, which have been shown to have antiseptic, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Quassia bidwillii include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve symptoms of fever, and support the immune system. Quassia bidwillii is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and can be used to make a bitter tonic or added to herbal remedies to enhance their effectiveness.
